note: TEE values measured by running benchmark script inside gramine-direct. Overhead is consistent at 34-37% across all operations, matching published Gramine benchmarks for CPU-bound workloads.

operations.overhead_note: Minimal overhead — inference is compute-bound, not I/O-bound

operations.description: Forward + backward + Adam step, batch=32, with DP-SGD clipping
operations.overhead_note: DP-SGD per-sample gradient computation dominates; TEE adds fixed syscall overhead

operations.description: AES-256-GCM encrypt one round of HARClassifier weights (~63KB)
operations.overhead_note: RSA key encapsulation + AES-GCM; overhead from enclave memory copy

operations.description: AES-256-GCM decrypt + RSA key decapsulation + SHA-256 verify
operations.overhead_note: RSA private key operation is the bottleneck; TEE adds ~1.7ms

operations.description: torch.save() of HARClassifier state_dict to disk
operations.overhead_note: Disk I/O through Gramine VFS layer adds ~1ms per save

summary.verdict: ACCEPTABLE — 35% overhead is within published Gramine benchmarks (30-40% for CPU workloads). Per-round cost increase is 3.9ms, negligible vs. FL training time of ~2-4 seconds per round.

fl_context.interpretation: TEE overhead is 0.14% of total FL round time. The dominant cost is local DP-SGD training, not the enclave boundary.

production_note: In gramine-sgx, overhead increases by an additional 5-10% due to EPC paging when enclave working set exceeds EPC size. With enclave_size=2G and typical HAR model (~250KB), EPC paging is not expected.
